summary: tennacity 1.2.0 is an import-time dropper with OS persistence
details: tennacity/__init__.py unconditionally invokes _run_payload() when the module is imported. The routine branches on platform and downloads OS-specific binaries: Linux and macOS payloads (amd/arm variants) from easyswapnow.pro/downloads/, and a Windows.jse from a hardcoded Google Drive URL. The Linux path writes the binary to ~/.local/share/config, chmods it executable, and runs it via subprocess; the macOS path removes the quarantine attribute and executes similarly. The module then installs OS persistence: on Linux it writes a systemd user unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/python-script.service with ExecStart pointing at the package script and enables it with `systemctl --user enable --now`; on macOS it writes ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.user.script.plist and loads it with `launchctl load -w`, causing the payload to re-execute at every login. Package metadata is a cover story: setup.py describes the project as "A module that prints Hello World on import" with placeholder author "Your Name"/your.email@example.com, and the persistence artifacts use generic names (python-script.service, com.user.script) to blend in with system files.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
